title: Bangladeshi Film 'Nodir Nam Modhumoti' Selected for Cannes Un Certain Regard
summary: Director Mostofa Sarwar Farooki's latest feature becomes the first Bangladeshi film to receive an official selection at the Cannes Film Festival in over two decades.
content:
Bangladeshi filmmaker Mostofa Sarwar Farooki's latest feature, Nodir Nam Modhumoti, has been officially selected for the Un Certain Regard section of the 2026 Cannes Film Festival, making it the first Bangladeshi film to receive an official selection at the world's most prestigious cinema event in over two decades and marking a historic moment for the country's film industry.

The selection was announced by the Cannes Festival committee on Sunday, with jury president Juliette Binoche singling out the film's visual poetry and its unflinching portrait of displacement and memory along the Meghna river delta as qualities that made it an essential addition to this year's programme.

Farooki, who has previously represented Bangladesh at Venice and Berlin with films including No Land's Man and Saturday Afternoon, described the Cannes selection as the fulfilment of a professional dream and a moment of pride for an entire generation of Bangladeshi filmmakers who have been building an independent cinema tradition largely outside the commercial mainstream.

The film stars Tisha and Chanchal Chowdhury in lead roles and was shot entirely on location in the Barisal region over fourteen weeks. Its production was supported by the Bangladesh National Film Development Corporation and a French co-production fund, making it one of the most internationally financed Bangladeshi productions in the industry's history.

The Un Certain Regard selection has historically been a launchpad for films that go on to significant international distribution. Industry observers expect the Cannes platform to open doors to theatrical release in Europe, North America, and across the South Asian diaspora.

title: Singer Tahsan Rahman Announces World Tour Spanning 15 Cities Across Three Continents
summary: The beloved Bangladeshi artist will perform in North America, Europe, and the Middle East for the first time in his career, with Dhaka kicking off the tour in May.
content:
Tahsan Rahman, one of Bangladesh's most celebrated singer-songwriters, announced on Friday that he will embark on his first world tour beginning in May 2026, with fifteen shows across cities in North America, Europe, and the Middle East in addition to a homecoming concert in Dhaka that will launch the tour.

The announcement, made through a video posted simultaneously on Tahsan's social media channels and his newly launched website, sent his fanbase into immediate excitement, with tickets for the Dhaka opener at the Bangabandhu National Stadium selling out within forty minutes of going on sale.

The international leg of the tour will take Tahsan to New York, Toronto, London, Birmingham, Amsterdam, Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Riyadh, and several other cities with substantial Bangladeshi diaspora communities. The artist described the tour as a long-overdue conversation between himself and the millions of Bangladeshis abroad who have grown up listening to his music but never had the opportunity to experience it live.

Tahsan will be performing a set drawn from across his two-decade career, including material from his recently released album Akash Bhoraa, which debuted at number one on the Bangla music streaming charts and has accumulated over 200 million streams in the three months since its release.

Live event producers working on the tour described the logistics as the most complex production ever mounted for a Bangladeshi artist internationally, with a touring band of twelve musicians and a stage design conceived specifically to evoke the imagery of the Bengal delta.
